name: Tri-Cities Community
description_short: Tri-Cities Community Health is a federally recognized community health center providing primary care, dental, pharmacy, and preventive health services across 6 locations with a sliding fee scale for uninsured and underinsured patients.
description_long:
Tri-Cities Community Health (TCCH) is a Health Center Program grantee and deemed Public Health Service institution serving the Tri-Cities region. The organization operates under a mission centered on the principle that people matter, providing quality healthcare regardless of patients' economic or insurance status. TCCH has established itself as a critical healthcare access point in its community for several decades, with a commitment to serving vulnerable and underserved populations.

The organization offers comprehensive whole-body care through multiple service lines including pediatric and adult primary care, dental services, pharmacy services, and specialist care for chronic conditions like diabetes management. Patients can request appointments, access a patient portal, pay bills online, and locate one of six physical locations. TCCH explicitly serves patients with or without insurance and offers sliding fee scale pricing for those who qualify, making healthcare more affordable for low-income residents.

What distinguishes TCCH is its explicit commitment to serving populations regardless of legal or economic status, combined with its federal recognition as a Public Health Service entity. The organization demonstrates community engagement through initiatives like providing dental care to homeless populations in partnership with Columbia Basin College and celebrating National Health Center Week. The integrated team-based approach to care coordination across multiple service lines reflects a patient-centered model rather than fragmented specialty care.

The primary limitation is that TCCH is fundamentally a healthcare provider, not a financial services or credit-related organization. While it addresses affordability through sliding scales and serves low-income populations, it does not provide payday alternatives, emergency loans, or any financial products. The categorization as a payday alternative appears to be an error, as the website contains no financial lending products, loan programs, or credit-building services of any kind.
services: Care for uninsured and underinsured patients
pros: Sliding fee scale available for uninsured and underinsured patients to reduce healthcare costs
Serves patients regardless of economic or legal status, addressing barriers to care
Comprehensive whole-body care with integrated specialists from pediatrics through adult care
Federally recognized Public Health Service grantee status ensures accountability and funding stability
Multiple access points with 6 locations across the Tri-Cities region
Online patient portal and bill payment system for convenient account management
On-site pharmacy services for convenient medication access
Community outreach programs including dental care for homeless populations
cons: Not a financial services provider—offers no loans, credit products, or emergency cash services
Miscategorized as a payday alternative when it is actually a healthcare provider
Website does not display specific pricing, fee schedules, or APR information
No information on wait times or appointment availability windows
Limited detail on specialist availability and referral processes
best_for: Uninsured and underinsured individuals seeking affordable primary care and preventive services
Families with children needing pediatric care and well-child visits
Low-income patients who qualify for sliding fee scale reductions
Patients seeking integrated dental, pharmacy, and primary care services in one system
diagnosis: Tri-Cities Community Health is a federally recognized community health center, not a financial services provider or payday alternative lender. It should be recategorized from 'payday-alternatives' to 'banking' (as the closest general financial access category, though 'healthcare' would be more accurate if available). This organization is best suited for low-income, uninsured, or underinsured individuals seeking affordable healthcare services with sliding fee scale pricing, not for consumers seeking emergency loans or credit products.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
